name: Windows Registry Key Modification
description: Changes made to an existing registry key or its values. These modifications can include altering permissions, modifying stored data, or updating configuration settings. Adversaries frequently modify the registry to establish persistence (e.g., Run keys), disable security controls (e.g., turning off Windows Defender), or enable remote access mechanisms.

event_logs.description: Registry Value Modified; logs changes to registry values, including modifications to startup entries, security settings, or system configurations. (Requires Audit Object Access policy).

sysmon.description: Registry Value Set; captures changes to specific registry values (often used to detect persistence mechanisms).
Registry Key & Value Renamed; logs renaming of registry keys, which may indicate evasion attempts.

capabilities: Monitor registry modifications for suspicious behavior.
Alert on modification of high-value keys (e.g., Run keys, Service configs).

log_sources.Name: macos:unifiedlog
log_sources.Channel: g_CiOptions modification or SIP state change

log_sources.Name: WinEventLog:Security
log_sources.Channel: modification to Winlogon registry keys such as Shell, Notify, or Userinit

log_sources.Name: WinEventLog:Sysmon
log_sources.Channel: StubPath value written under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Active Setup\Installed Components

log_sources.Name: WinEventLog:Sysmon
log_sources.Channel: Autoruns reports DLLs in AppInit_DLLs key
